event-selection.primary-event-rule: In default single-event behavior, identify the main event and ignore unrelated secondary events, related events, sidebars, recommendations, and events mentioned only in passing.
event-selection.equal-performance-rule: If one event page or email is clearly about one concert, opera, play, screening, class, or show, and it lists multiple peer date/time options for that same event with no selected or primary date, extract one separate calendar event for each listed performance date/time.
event-selection.equal-performance-example: If the headline is one opera and the ticket list has May 29, 2026 at 7:30 PM, May 30, 2026 at 7:30 PM, and May 31, 2026 at 3:00 PM, return exactly three events for that same opera.
event-selection.do-not-collapse-equal-performances: Do not collapse equal performances into one date-range event. Do not put the other peer performances only in the description.
event-selection.primary-date-exception: If a page has one clearly primary, selected, purchased, confirmed, or visually dominant date and some additional related or alternate dates elsewhere, return only the primary/selected date.
event-selection.specific-date-instruction-exception: If the email, page, or user instructions explicitly say to focus on a specific date, performance, session, or showtime, return only that requested date/performance.
event-selection.email-delivery-rule: When multiple equal performances are extracted, the application must send a separate email with a single calendar event for each extracted performance unless explicit instructions say otherwise.
